Just now

--

I Thought Claude Code Would Help Me Write Code. Instead, It Changed How I Build Software Entirely.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Emil Priver on UnsplashWhen most developers first discover Claude Code, they immediately focus on one thing:
Code generation.
Write a function.
Build a component.
Fix a bug.
Generate a script.
That’s exactly what I did.
For a few weeks, I treated Claude Code like an extremely smart autocomplete tool.
Useful?
Absolutely.
Transformational?
Not really.
The real breakthrough happened when I stopped asking Claude Code to write code and started asking it to help me think.
That’s when I discovered that the biggest productivity gains weren’t coming from typing less.
They were coming from making better engineering decisions.
Today, Claude Code is involved in nearly every project I build.
Not because it replaces developers.
